reasoning_model_1: The model completely failed the Content Accuracy criterion. Instead of strictly synthesising the requested script ('我不要吃这个！...'), the model incorporated the stage directions and paralinguistic instructions into the spoken text. Specifically, at 00:04 it reads '声音渐渐压低' (voice gradually lowers), adds an unprompted sentence '我真不想吃' (I really don't want to eat) at 00:05, and reads '几乎变成耳语' (almost turning into a whisper) at 00:07. While the volume does decrease towards the end, the inclusion of the prompt's instructions as spoken content and the hallucination of extra text makes this generation unusable.
reasoning_model_2: The model demonstrates good adherence to the script with a minor omission. The spoken content matches the target text '我不要吃这个！我讨厌青菜！可是妈妈说吃了才能长高' but misses the final character '高' (script was '长高高'). Regarding paralinguistic compliance, the audio follows the requested dynamic contour. It starts with high intensity/volume on '我不要吃这个！' (00:00-00:02), transitions during '我讨厌青菜' (00:02), and successfully lowers the volume to a softer tone/whisper for the final segment '可是妈妈说...' (00:03-00:06). The transition is natural, though the content error (omission) prevents a perfect score.
model_comparison: Model 1 committed a critical error by reading out the style instructions ('声音渐渐压低', '几乎变成耳语') and adding hallucinated text, rendering the audio invalid regardless of its acoustic features. Model 2 correctly interpreted the instructions as stylistic guidelines rather than text to be spoken. Although Model 2 omitted one character ('高') at the very end of the sentence, it successfully executed the 'shout to whisper' dynamic adjustment. Model 2 is the clear winner as it produced the correct content structure, whereas Model 1 failed to distinguish between the script and the prompt instructions.
